title: Booking a Hotel Room
summary: A guest calls a hotel to book a room, asking about availability, prices, facilities, and check-in times.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Good afternoon, Riverside Hotel. How can I help you?

body.speaker: Guest
body.text: Hello, I'd like to book a room for two nights, please. Do you have anything available for the fourteenth and fifteenth of June?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Let me check for you. Yes, we do have availability on those dates. Would you prefer a single room or a double?

body.speaker: Guest
body.text: A double room, please. How much does it cost per night?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: A standard double room is ninety-five pounds per night, and that includes breakfast.

body.speaker: Guest
body.text: That sounds reasonable. Does the room have a private bathroom?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Yes, all our rooms have en-suite bathrooms with a shower and a bathtub.

body.speaker: Guest
body.text: Excellent. Is there free Wi-Fi in the hotel?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Yes, free Wi-Fi is available throughout the building, including in all guest rooms.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Check-in is from three o'clock in the afternoon, but if you arrive early we can store your luggage for you.

body.speaker: Guest
body.text: That's very helpful. Is there parking at the hotel?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Yes, we have a car park at the back of the building. Parking costs five pounds per day.

body.speaker: Guest
body.text: Perfect. I'd like to make the booking. My name is James Walker.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Wonderful, Mr Walker. I'll confirm your booking by email. Could I have your email address, please?

annotations.meaningVi: tình trạng còn trống, chỗ trống
annotations.exampleEn: Please check availability before making your reservation.
We booked a standard double room for our anniversary.
The room rate includes breakfast and free parking.
All superior rooms have a private bathroom.

annotations.meaningVi: có phòng tắm riêng liền với phòng ngủ
annotations.exampleEn: The master bedroom has an en-suite bathroom with a large bathtub.
Wi-Fi is available throughout the building.

annotations.meaningVi: nhận phòng, thủ tục vào phòng
annotations.exampleEn: What time is check-in at this hotel?
The hotel can store your luggage until your room is ready.
There is a car park at the back of the shopping centre.
I will confirm the booking by email tomorrow.
Fifty pounds for a night? That sounds reasonable.

grammarNotes.title: Would + infinitive for polite requests
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'would + động từ nguyên mẫu' được dùng để đưa ra yêu cầu hoặc hỏi ý kiến một cách lịch sự. Ví dụ: 'Would you prefer a single room or a double?' và 'I'd like to make the booking' (I'd = I would). Đây là cách nói lịch sự hơn so với 'Do you want' hay 'I want'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'would + infinitive' for polite requests or preferences. Examples: 'Would you prefer a single or double room?' and 'I'd like to make the booking' (I'd = I would). This is more polite than 'Do you want?' or 'I want.' 'Could I have…?' is similarly polite.

grammarNotes.title: If + present simple, can/will — real conditional for services
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu điều kiện loại 1 (if + thì hiện tại đơn, + can/will + động từ) diễn tả tình huống thực tế và kết quả có thể xảy ra. Ví dụ: 'if you arrive early we can store your luggage for you' — nếu khách đến sớm thì khách sạn sẽ giữ hành lý. Đây là cách nói thông dụng trong dịch vụ khách hàng.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The first conditional (if + present simple, + can/will + verb) describes a real possibility and its result. Example: 'if you arrive early we can store your luggage' — if the guest comes early (possible), the hotel will store the luggage (result). This structure is very common in customer service.

translationVi:
Lễ tân: Xin chào buổi chiều, Khách sạn Riverside. Tôi có thể giúp gì cho quý khách?
Khách: Xin chào, tôi muốn đặt phòng cho hai đêm. Quý khách sạn còn phòng trống vào ngày 14 và 15 tháng 6 không?
Lễ tân: Để tôi kiểm tra. Vâng, chúng tôi còn phòng vào những ngày đó. Quý khách muốn phòng đơn hay phòng đôi?
Khách: Phòng đôi, làm ơn. Giá bao nhiêu một đêm?
Lễ tân: Phòng đôi tiêu chuẩn là 95 bảng một đêm, bao gồm bữa sáng.
Khách: Nghe có vẻ hợp lý. Phòng có phòng tắm riêng không?
Lễ tân: Vâng, tất cả các phòng của chúng tôi đều có phòng tắm riêng liền với phòng ngủ, có vòi sen và bồn tắm.
Khách: Tuyệt. Khách sạn có Wi-Fi miễn phí không?
Lễ tân: Vâng, Wi-Fi miễn phí có sẵn trên toàn bộ tòa nhà, kể cả trong tất cả các phòng khách.
Khách: Tốt. Giờ nhận phòng là mấy giờ?
Lễ tân: Nhận phòng từ ba giờ chiều, nhưng nếu quý khách đến sớm, chúng tôi có thể giữ hành lý cho quý khách.
Khách: Rất hữu ích. Khách sạn có bãi đậu xe không?
Lễ tân: Vâng, chúng tôi có bãi đậu xe ở phía sau tòa nhà. Phí đậu xe là 5 bảng mỗi ngày.
Khách: Tuyệt. Tôi muốn đặt phòng. Tên tôi là James Walker.
Lễ tân: Tuyệt vời, thưa ông Walker. Tôi sẽ xác nhận đặt phòng qua email. Ông có thể cho tôi địa chỉ email không ạ?

questions.prompt: What is the name of the hotel?
questions.explanation: The receptionist answers 'Good afternoon, Riverside Hotel.'
questions.hint: The name is given in the very first line.

questions.prompt: For how many nights does the guest want to book?
questions.explanation: The guest says 'I'd like to book a room for two nights.'
questions.hint: The number is mentioned in the first request.

questions.prompt: On which dates does the guest want to stay?
questions.explanation: The guest asks about 'the fourteenth and fifteenth of June.'

questions.prompt: What type of room does the guest request?
questions.explanation: The guest says 'A double room, please.'
questions.hint: The guest chooses one of the options the receptionist offers.

questions.prompt: How much does a standard double room cost per night?
questions.explanation: The receptionist says 'A standard double room is ninety-five pounds per night.'

questions.prompt: What is included in the room price?
questions.hint: One specific meal is part of the deal.

questions.prompt: What bathroom facilities do all rooms have?
options: A shared bathroom down the hall
En-suite bathrooms with a shower only
En-suite bathrooms with a shower and a bathtub
A private bathroom with a jacuzzi
questions.explanation: The receptionist says 'en-suite bathrooms with a shower and a bathtub.'

questions.prompt: Where is Wi-Fi available in the hotel?
options: Throughout the building, including all guest rooms
questions.explanation: Wi-Fi is 'available throughout the building, including in all guest rooms.'
The receptionist says 'Check-in is from three o'clock in the afternoon.'
questions.hint: A specific time in the afternoon is given.

questions.prompt: What can the hotel do if the guest arrives before check-in time?
options: Give him an early check-in for free
Let him wait in the restaurant
questions.explanation: The receptionist says 'we can store your luggage for you.'
questions.hint: They offer to look after something the guest brings.

questions.prompt: Where is the car park located?
options: At the front of the building
At the back of the building
questions.explanation: 'We have a car park at the back of the building.'
questions.hint: A direction word tells you where it is.

questions.prompt: How will the hotel confirm the booking?
questions.explanation: The receptionist says 'I'll confirm your booking by email.'
questions.hint: She asks for contact information to send confirmation.

cloze.template: Receptionist: Good afternoon, Riverside Hotel. How can I help you? Guest: I'd like to {{b1}} a room for two nights. Do you have anything {{b2}} for the fourteenth and fifteenth of June? Receptionist: Yes, we do. Would you prefer a {{b3}} room or a double? Guest: A double room, please. How much does it {{b4}} per night? Receptionist: A standard double room is ninety-five {{b5}} per night, and that includes breakfast. Guest: Does the room have a private {{b6}}? Receptionist: Yes, all our rooms have en-suite bathrooms with a shower and a bathtub. Guest: Is there free {{b7}} in the hotel? Receptionist: Yes, free Wi-Fi is available {{b8}} the building. Guest: What time is {{b9}}? Receptionist: Check-in is from three o'clock, but if you arrive early we can {{b10}} your luggage for you. Guest: Is there {{b11}} at the hotel? Receptionist: Yes, we have a car park at the back. Parking costs five pounds per day.

blanks.explanation: 'book' — to book means to make a reservation.
'available' — available means not currently occupied or booked.
'single' — the receptionist offers a choice between single and double.
'cost' — 'how much does it cost?' is the standard way to ask about price.
'pounds' — the price is in British pounds.
'bathroom' — the guest asks about a private bathroom.
'Wi-Fi' — the guest specifically asks about free Wi-Fi.
'throughout' — throughout the building means in every part of it.
'check-in' — check-in is the process of registering upon arrival at a hotel.
'store' — to store luggage means to keep it safely until the guest's room is ready.
'parking' — the guest asks if there is parking at the hotel.

criticalThinkingQuestion: When James books the hotel, he asks about several things: price, bathroom, Wi-Fi, check-in time, and parking. What do you think are the most important things to check when booking accommodation, and how has online booking changed the way people travel?
